# HMS (Hospital Management System) - Project Specification

## Generated by AI from Planning Prompt

---

## 1. Complete Feature List

| Portal | Main Features | Restriction |
|---|---|---|
| **Laboratory** | Search patient by name, mobile, patient code, visit number. View ordered tests. Print test slip and test history. View today's test queue. Print log. | Read-only. No create, edit, delete. |
| **Reception** | Patient registration. Visit and admission entry. Billing for test, admission, doctor visit, doctor bill, cesarean or operation bill, medicine bill. Discount. Full and partial payment. Due tracking. Bill settlement by Current, Due, Fire Service. Expense entry. Investment entry. Daily cash and collection view. Print invoice, receipt, patient slip. | Edit rights limited to reception scope. |
| **Pharmacy** | Medicine master. Supplier master. Purchase entry. Batch and expiry tracking. Stock in and stock out. Low stock alert. Expiry alert. Pharmacy billing. Total investment and total income view. Print pharmacy invoice. | No access to reception finance or salaries. |
| **Managing Director** | Full dashboard. Daily income and daily expense. Staff setup. Salary entry and payment. Shareholder setup. Monthly profit closing. Share distribution. Approvals. User management. Settings. Audit logs. | Full control. |
| **Director** | Same portal as Managing Director, but usually report-focused or approval-based. Daily finance, salary reports, profit reports, shareholder reports. | Limited edit rights unless approved by policy. |

### Common System Features
- Role-based access
- Password login
- Optional two-step verification for management
- Search and filter on every major screen
- Printable A4 layouts
- Daily/weekly/monthly reports
- Audit trail
- Backup and restore
- Activity history for every financial action

### Reports
- Daily, weekly, monthly income
- Expenses
- Due collection
- Bill settlement
- Pharmacy sales
- Medicine stock
- Staff salary payments
- Profit statement
- Shareholder distribution

---

## 2. Database Schema

> **Important:** All money fields should be stored as integer poisha, not floating-point numbers.

### Core Relationships

```
patients -> visits -> invoices -> payments
visits -> lab_orders -> lab_order_items
invoices -> invoice_items
medicines -> medicine_stock_batches -> medicine_stock_movements
staff -> salary_payments
profit_periods -> profit_distributions -> shareholders
```

### Security and Access

```sql
roles(
  id, name, description
)

users(
  id, username, password_hash, full_name, role_id, staff_id,
  is_active, last_login_at, created_at
)

auth_sessions(
  id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at
)

audit_logs(
  id, user_id, module, action, entity_name, entity_id,
  before_json, after_json, ip_address, created_at
)
```

### Master Data

```sql
staff(
  id, name, address, position, salary,
  bank_account_number, mobile_number, join_date, is_active
)

doctors(
  id, name, specialty, mobile_number, consultation_fee, is_active
)

lab_tests(
  id, code, name, category, price, is_active
)

medicines(
  id, code, name, generic_name, unit,
  sale_price, reorder_level, is_active
)

suppliers(
  id, name, mobile_number, address, notes
)

expense_categories(
  id, name, is_active
)

settlement_types(
  id, code, name, is_active
)
```

**Seed `settlement_types` with:** `current`, `due`, `fire_service`

```sql
settings(
  key, value_json
)
```

### Patient and Visit Data

```sql
patients(
  id, patient_code, registration_serial, name,
  father_or_husband_name, address, mobile_number,
  guardian_mobile, gender, date_of_birth,
  created_by, created_at
)

visits(
  id, patient_id, visit_no, doctor_id, visit_type,
  admission_flag, admission_no, admission_date,
  discharge_date, notes, created_by, created_at
)

visit_services(
  id, visit_id, service_type, description, doctor_id,
  amount, reference_type, reference_id, created_at
)
```

**Recommended `service_type` values:** `test`, `admission`, `doctor_visit`, `doctor_bill_other`, `doctor_bill_operation`, `medicine`

### Laboratory

```sql
lab_orders(
  id, order_no, patient_id, visit_id,
  ordered_by, order_date, print_count, last_printed_at
)

lab_order_items(
  id, lab_order_id, lab_test_id,
  unit_price, discount, line_total
)
```

### Billing and Finance

```sql
invoices(
  id, invoice_no, patient_id, visit_id, module,
  subtotal, discount_total, total_amount,
  paid_amount, due_amount, status,
  created_by, created_at
)

invoice_items(
  id, invoice_id, item_category, description,
  quantity, unit_price, line_total,
  service_id, stock_batch_id
)

payments(
  id, receipt_no, invoice_id, settlement_type_id,
  payment_method, amount, payment_date,
  notes, received_by
)

expenses(
  id, expense_no, expense_date, expense_category_id,
  description, amount, payment_method, created_by
)

investments(
  id, investment_no, module, investment_date,
  description, amount, source_name,
  payment_method, created_by
)

daily_finance_snapshots(
  id, snapshot_date,
  test_income,
  operation_doctor_bills,
  other_doctor_bills,
  pharmacy_income,
  total_income,
  total_expense,
  generated_at
)
```

### Pharmacy

```sql
medicine_purchases(
  id, purchase_no, supplier_id, purchase_date,
  subtotal, discount_total, total_amount,
  paid_amount, due_amount, created_by
)

medicine_purchase_items(
  id, purchase_id, medicine_id, batch_no,
  expiry_date, quantity, purchase_price,
  sale_price, line_total
)

medicine_stock_batches(
  id, medicine_id, batch_no, expiry_date,
  quantity_received, quantity_available,
  purchase_item_id
)

medicine_stock_movements(
  id, medicine_id, batch_id, movement_type,
  quantity, unit_cost, unit_price,
  reference_type, reference_id,
  movement_date, created_by
)
```

### Salary and Profit Sharing

```sql
salary_payments(
  id, staff_id, pay_month, pay_year,
  basic_salary, bonus, deduction, net_salary,
  payment_date, payment_method, reference_no, created_by
)

shareholders(
  id, name, address, mobile_number,
  share_type, share_count, start_date, is_active
)

profit_periods(
  id, month, year,
  total_income, total_expense, net_profit,
  holders_percent, owners_percent,
  holders_share_count, owners_share_count,
  holders_pool_amount, owners_pool_amount,
  status, approved_by, approved_at
)

profit_distributions(
  id, profit_period_id, shareholder_id,
  share_count, per_share_amount,
  distribution_amount, paid_status,
  paid_date, notes
)
```

### File Storage

```sql
attachments(
  id, entity_name, entity_id,
  file_name, r2_key, mime_type,
  uploaded_by, created_at
)
```

## 3. User Interface Structure

### Common (All Portals)
- Login
- Change password
- Dashboard
- Search bar
- Notifications
- Print center
- Profile
- Logout

### Laboratory Portal
- Dashboard
- Today's test queue
- Patient test search
- Test detail view
- Print test slip
- Print history

### Reception Portal
- Dashboard
- New patient registration
- Patient list
- New visit or admission
- Create invoice
- Payment collection
- Due settlement
- Expense entry
- Investment entry
- Settlement register
- Receipt print
- Daily reports

### Pharmacy Portal
- Dashboard
- Medicine list
- Supplier list
- Purchase entry
- Stock and batch view
- Pharmacy sale
- Low stock and expiry alerts
- Income and investment report
- Print invoice

### Managing Director and Director Portal
- Executive dashboard
- Daily income report
- Daily expense report
- Staff list
- Salary payment
- Profit calculation
- Shareholder register
- Distribution register
- Reports
- Audit log
- User management
- Settings

---

## 4. Cloudflare-Native Tech Stack

| Layer | Tech | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Frontend** | React on Cloudflare Pages | Fast portal interface |
| **Backend API** | Cloudflare Workers with Hono | Role-based API and business logic |
| **Database** | Cloudflare D1 | Main relational database |
| **Counters & Stock** | Durable Objects | Invoice numbers, stock deduction, daily counter control |
| **File Storage** | Cloudflare R2 | Reports, exports, attachments, backups |
| **Background Jobs** | Cloudflare Queues | Report generation, backup jobs, notifications |
| **Scheduled Tasks** | Cron Triggers or Workflows | Daily summaries, monthly profit closing, automatic backups |
| **Cache & Config** | Cloudflare KV | Small config and fast lookups |
| **Security** | Turnstile, WAF, Rate Limiting, Zero Trust | Login protection and portal security |
| **Monitoring** | Workers Analytics Engine, Logpush | Usage and error monitoring |

### Recommended Architecture
- React frontend on Pages
- Workers API
- D1 database
- R2 file storage
- Durable Objects for counters and stock
- Queues for reports and backup

---

## 5. Profit-Sharing Logic

### Settings Values
- **Share value** = 100,000 Taka
- **Total shares** = 300
- **Profit-holder shares** = 100
- **Owner shares** = 200
- **Profit-holder pool** = 30%
- **Owner pool** = 70%

### Monthly Formula

```
Net Profit = Total Income - Total Expenses
Profit Holder Pool = Net Profit * 30%
Owner Pool = Net Profit * 70%

Holder Per Share = Profit Holder Pool / Total Profit-Holder Shares
Owner Per Share = Owner Pool / Total Owner Shares

Distribution Amount = Per Share Amount * Share Count
```

> This should be system-calculated, not manually typed.

---

## 6. Security and Backup

- Screen-level and API-level permission checks
- Never allow hard delete on invoices, payments, salary, or profit records
- Full audit logs for create, edit, delete, print, and settlement actions
- Daily D1 backup to R2
- Weekly and monthly snapshots
- Stronger authentication for management logins

---

## 7. Development Timeline Estimate

**Team:** 2 full-stack developers, 1 tester, 1 designer/project lead

| Phase | Time |
|---|---:|
| Requirement finalization and workflow mapping | 1-2 weeks |
| UI design and database design | 1-2 weeks |
| Authentication, roles, audit log, settings | 1-2 weeks |
| Reception module | 3 weeks |
| Laboratory module | 1 week |
| Pharmacy module | 3 weeks |
| Managing Director and Director finance, salary, profit sharing | 2-3 weeks |
| Reports, print layouts, backup, hardening | 2 weeks |
| Testing, user acceptance, training, deployment | 2 weeks |

### Total Estimated Time
- **Production-ready first version:** 16-18 weeks
- **Lighter first release:** 10-12 weeks (salary & profit-sharing in phase 2)

---

## 8. Important Notes

- **"Fire Service"** is best treated as a configurable settlement type so the accounting rule can be changed later without code changes
- All money fields should be stored as **integer poisha** (not floating-point) for accuracy
- Use batch-based stock with expiry-aware deduction in pharmacy
- Daily finance snapshots for fast reporting
